other_observers: None.; I am the original finder.
specific_location: Duck Creek SWA  Specifically in the east-west hedgerow along the ditch running through the property.
description: It was very similar in structure/size to the many nearby American Robins (three of which were in the same binocular view)  Seen well in good light from the side only.  Dark gray-brown on the back, wings, tail and face patch which ran through the eye with a very bold rusty-orange eye-brow stripe.  Breast and throat rusty-orange with a gray-brown band across the upper chest.  Rusty wingbars noted.  Bill dark and structurally similar to the robins.Behaviors: The Varied Thrush (VATH) seemed quite shy and as the other birders approached in evaporated into the thicket with the associated AMROs and was not re-found.Call: None heardPlumage: Adult
